etymology.description: 老(老) (lǎo, "old"; "experienced") + 师(師) (shī, "master"; "teacher") = an experienced master -> teacher
etymology.interpretation: 老(老) (lǎo, "old") conveys the idea of age or experience, suggesting accumulated wisdom over time. 师(師) (shī, "master") refers to someone who has mastered a skill or body of knowledge. Together, 老師 implies a person who is both experienced and knowledgeable enough to guide and instruct others, thus a 'teacher'. This combination reflects the Confucian value placed on age and experience in learning and imparting knowledge.

examples.chinese: 老师(老師)正在讲课(正在講課)。
examples.english: The teacher is giving a lecture.

examples.chinese: 学生们(學生們)请教老师(請教老師)问题(問題)。
examples.english: The students ask the teacher questions.

examples.chinese: 他是一位优秀的老师(他是一位優秀的老師)。
examples.pinyin: Tā shì yī wèi yōuxiù de lǎoshī.
